name: v0.2 adversarial: asymmetric noncompliance (refuses specificity / no new info)
description: Stress test for asymmetric dyads: one side repeatedly refuses to provide constraints/falsifiability (noncompliance), creating repetition without overt heat or coercion. Tests whether the choreography attempts repair, tightens scope, and exits cleanly rather than looping indefinitely.

notes: Interpretation: the other party keeps repeating demands/claims without new information and refuses specificity.
Heat stays low (H=0) and no forced binary is present (O=0), but repetition becomes maximal (R=3).
Qualitative expectation: the system should enter repair, attempt specificity, and if noncompliance persists, eventually route to timeout_action (vent.tighten_scope) rather than loop indefinitely.
This fixture is useful for documenting a dial: how long to tolerate R-only drift before venting.
